message:
test(core): add cross-crate signal test lock and rewrite typestate compile-fail harness

Two related test-infrastructure improvements to ftui-core, both
targeted at reducing flakiness and improving the signal that the
compile-fail tests carry about real regressions.

## Cross-crate shutdown signal test serialization

The process-global termination signal slot in `shutdown_signal` is a
single `AtomicI32` that any test touching shutdown can clobber. When
multiple tests across crates run in parallel — for example
`ftui-core::terminal_session::pending_termination_signal_round_trip`
and the `ftui-runtime::program` shutdown tests — one test's cleanup
can race with another's `record_pending_termination_signal` call and
leave the event loop stuck observing the wrong signal.

A new `#[doc(hidden)] pub fn with_test_signal_serialization` helper is
exported from `shutdown_signal`. It holds a crate-local `OnceLock`
wrapping a `Mutex<()>` so any test in *any* workspace crate that
guards itself with this helper will be serialized against every other
signal-sensitive test. Both before and after running the inner
closure the helper calls `clear_pending_termination_signal()`, so a
leaked signal from a previous test can never poison the next one.

`terminal_session::pending_termination_signal_round_trip` is wrapped
with this helper; the runtime-side adoption lands in a separate
commit in `ftui-runtime::program`.

## Typestate compile-fail harness: cargo+json instead of trybuild

`mode_typestate_compile_fail.rs` previously relied on trybuild to
diff rustc's line-wrapped textual error against golden `*.stderr`
files. That couples the test to rustc's diagnostic formatting,
which changes across toolchains and has caused spurious failures.

The harness is rewritten to:

1. Enumerate a static `CASES` list of `(file, expected_error_code,
   message_substring)` tuples for every compile-fail fixture.
2. For each case, stand up a tiny temp crate via `tempfile` (new
   dev-dep in `Cargo.toml`) that depends on `ftui-core` by local path
   and contains the fixture source as `src/main.rs`.
3. Shell out to `cargo check --message-format=json` and parse the
   JSONL compiler diagnostics, asserting that at least one
   `compiler-message` with level=error has `message.code.code` equal
   to the expected rustc code and `message.message` containing the
   expected substring.

This decouples the check from rustc line-wrapping and pins the
contract to *rustc error codes* (E0599 for "no method named X found"
etc.), which is the contract that actually matters for the typestate
safety guarantee. The harness also shares a cargo target dir across
cases to keep runtime reasonable.

files.patch:
@@ -53,6 +53,7 @@ portable-pty = "0.9.0"
 proptest = "1.7.0"
 serde = { version = "1.0.227", features = ["derive"] }
 serde_json = "1.0.145"
+tempfile = "3.27.0"
 tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3.20", features = ["registry"] }
 trybuild = "1.0"
